255:43236.name: Homepage There are core features of mobile banking that can easily meet basic requirements. Non-core (More) features There are additional payment functions for life, purchasing financial products and insurance, etc.
255:43236.characters:
Homepage
There are core features of mobile banking that can easily meet basic requirements.
Non-core (More) features
There are additional payment functions for life, purchasing financial products and insurance, etc.

2026:224.name: 3. British users have higher Perceived Security of Bank Card Using than Chinese users. 4. Fewer steps to manage card security in Lloyds Bank 5. British and Chinese users do not agree two sets of icons (Homepage & Non-core features) represent the same function for each. Cognitive differences in these two sets of icons. 6. The Lloyds mobile banking automatically enables facial recognition to log in, while The Bank of China mobile banking needs to manually find the account first and click the login button. 7. Lloyds Mobile Banking gives British users a better experience for clean and simple interface. 8. Lloyds Mobile Banking has greater Efficiency and Effectiveness.
2026:224.characters:
3. British users have higher Perceived Security of Bank Card Using than Chinese users.
4. Fewer steps to manage card security in Lloyds Bank
5. British and Chinese users do not agree two sets of icons (Homepage & Non-core features) represent the same function for each. Cognitive differences in these two sets of icons.
6. The Lloyds mobile banking automatically enables facial recognition to log in, while The Bank of China      mobile banking needs to manually find the account first and click the login button.
7. Lloyds Mobile Banking gives British users a better experience for clean and simple interface.
8. Lloyds Mobile Banking has greater Efficiency and Effectiveness.

2026:218.name: 2. Both British and Chinese users consider mobile banking is convenient and fast for transferring money, and domestic money transfer are used frequently in both countries. However, British users have higher requirement for domestic money transfer.
2026:218.characters: 2. Both British and Chinese users consider mobile banking is convenient and fast for transferring money, and domestic money transfer are used frequently in both countries. However, British users have higher requirement for domestic money transfer.

253:41673.name: The emergence of smart mobile devices makes mobile banking get great development, but Different Cultural Backgrounds make mobile banking in China and the UK present their own Uniqueness in UI and UX. These differences further stimulate interest in exploration and research.

244:36993.name: With the development of mobile banking, User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design of mobile banking have their own uniqueness according to the different user requirements in Chinese and British cultural backgrounds. This study investigates the influence of cultural differences between China and the UK on UX design of mobile banking, taking Lloyds Bank and Bank of China mobile banking as examples.
244:36993.characters: With the development of mobile banking, User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design of mobile banking have their own uniqueness according to the different user requirements in Chinese and British cultural backgrounds. This study investigates the influence of cultural differences between China and the UK on UX design of mobile banking, taking Lloyds Bank and Bank of China mobile banking as examples.

2001:393.name: Using Qualtrics, SPSS 28.0 and Excel as analysis tools, descriptive statistical and t-test analysis were performed on the collected data.

2026:172.name: 1. British users used Lloyds Mobile Banking More Frequently than bank of China mobile banking used by Chinese users. Meanwhile, British users have higher requirements for balance checking, and 100% of British users prefer mobile banking services. Balance checking is placed in the homepage in Lloyd’s interface Mobile banking has a certain irreplaceable role in the UK (Alipay in China)
2026:172.characters:
1. British users used Lloyds Mobile Banking More Frequently than bank of China mobile banking used by Chinese users. Meanwhile, British users have higher requirements for balance checking, and 100% of British users prefer mobile banking services.
Balance checking is placed in the homepage in Lloyd’s interface
Mobile banking has a certain irreplaceable role in the UK (Alipay in China)

There are core features of mobile banking that can easily meet basic requirements.
Non-core (More) features
There are additional payment functions for life, purchasing financial products and insurance, etc.

2026:184.name: 3. British users have higher Perceived Security of Bank Card Using than Chinese users. 4. Fewer steps to manage card security in Lloyds Bank 5. British and Chinese users do not agree two sets of icons (Homepage & Non-core features) represent the same function for each. Cognitive differences in these two sets of icons. 6. The Lloyds mobile banking automatically enables facial recognition to log in, while The Bank of China mobile banking needs to manually find the account first and click the login button. 7. Lloyds Mobile Banking gives British users a better experience for clean and simple interface. 8. Lloyds Mobile Banking has greater Efficiency and Effectiveness.
2026:184.characters:
3. British users have higher Perceived Security of Bank Card Using than Chinese users.
4. Fewer steps to manage card security in Lloyds Bank
5. British and Chinese users do not agree two sets of icons (Homepage & Non-core features) represent the same function for each. Cognitive differences in these two sets of icons.
6. The Lloyds mobile banking automatically enables facial recognition to log in, while The Bank of China      mobile banking needs to manually find the account first and click the login button.
7. Lloyds Mobile Banking gives British users a better experience for clean and simple interface.
8. Lloyds Mobile Banking has greater Efficiency and Effectiveness.

255:42455.name: Lack of cross-cultural studies which compare different user requirements of mobile banking users in China and the UK and user experience design of the apps.
255:42455.characters: Lack of cross-cultural studies which compare different user requirements of mobile banking users in China and the UK and user experience design of the apps.

255:42986.name: The questionnaire consisted of 19 questions and contained four parts: In order to collect the data of Chinese users and British users separately, an English questionnaire was first designed and it was subsequently translated into a Chinese questionnaire. After translation, researchers reviewed the English and Chinese versions of the questionnaires in order to verify that the questions were equivalent between the two languages.
255:42986.characters:

2026:212.name: 1. British users used Lloyds Mobile Banking More Frequently than bank of China mobile banking used by Chinese users. Meanwhile, British users have higher requirements for balance checking, and 100% of British users prefer mobile banking services. Balance checking is placed in the homepage in Lloyd’s interface Mobile banking has a certain irreplaceable role in the UK (Alipay in China)

258:53988.name: A cross-cultural study on user experience design in mobile banking
258:53988.characters: A cross-cultural study on  user experience design in mobile banking

244:36660.name: With the development of mobile banking, User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design of mobile banking have their own uniqueness according to the different user requirements in Chinese and British cultural backgrounds. This study investigates the influence of cultural differences between China and the UK on UX design of mobile banking, taking Lloyds Bank and Bank of China mobile banking as examples.
244:36660.characters: With the development of mobile banking, User Interface (UI) and User Experience (UX) design of mobile banking have their own uniqueness according to the different user requirements in Chinese and British cultural backgrounds. This study investigates the influence of cultural differences between China and the UK on UX design of mobile banking, taking Lloyds Bank and Bank of China mobile banking as examples.
